input.title: The Fire Line
input.description: A documentary following three wildfire crews across the western United States, Australia, and Portugal during a single fire season, examining how climate change is creating a permanent fire economy and reshaping rural communities on three continents.
input.in_place: US crew attached, National Geographic development funding, one US fire crew confirmed
input.australian_angle: One of three storylines follows an Australian CFA crew in Victoria; Australia holds all Australasian rights in proposed structure
input.rights_offered: All Australasian rights; shared credit as co-producer

input.title: Deep Water
input.description: A documentary about free divers who train without equipment in open ocean.

input.title: The Crossing
input.description: A documentary following refugees and aid workers at three Mediterranean crossing points — the Strait of Gibraltar, the Lampedusa route, and the Aegean passage between Turkey and Greece — over one year. The film interweaves observational footage from rescue operations with intimate portraits of people waiting on both sides of the sea.
input.co_production_structure: Majority German / minority Italian co-production under the German-Italian bilateral co-production treaty
input.in_place: ZDF (Germany) as lead broadcaster, BKM (German Federal Film Fund) development support, director with prior Mediterranean access
input.italian_angle: The Lampedusa storyline constitutes approximately 40% of the series; filming on Lampedusa and in Sicilian reception centres requires Italian co-production partner for access and credibility
input.rights_offered: Italian and Swiss Italian-language rights; co-producer credit; editorial consultation on Lampedusa storyline
